mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the hazard ratio for negative returns after the threshold breach falls below 1.2.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity and credit-sensitive demand, which compresses equity multiples and yields negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where the CPIAUCSL index exceeds 300, aggressive federal funds rate hikes trigger a liquidity shock that disproportionately penalizes duration-sensitive growth stocks, producing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive or statistically flat when both CPI and FEDFUNDS rise concurrently, or the cross-sectional momentum of rate-hike months shows no dispersion in equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect increasing business confidence and credit availability, which historically precede positive corporate earnings revisions and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between monthly HOUST changes and 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.10, or forward returns in rising housing periods are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls consistently exceed 156000 for three consecutive months, it indicates an overheating labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, ultimately capping equity upside and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative following three consecutive months of high payroll growth, or the predictive Rank IC for this threshold rule exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising real estate activity and elevated federal funds rates creates a credit crunch dynamic where higher borrowing costs offset housing demand, leading to margin compression and negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
regime_hint: credit-sensitive demand under tight monetary policy
failure_condition: The interaction model fails to outperform a single-factor FEDFUNDS model in out-of-sample R-squared, or quarterly forward returns do not decline when HOUST rises alongside FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where industrial production growth outpaces real GDP growth, supply chain bottlenecks ease and corporate margins expand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon as earnings revisions accelerate.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth rate > GDPC1 growth rate
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO growth exceeds GDPC1 growth, or the cross-sectional dispersion in equity returns across supply-demand divergence deciles shows no monotonic pattern.
